description: Consolidated inventory of remote operational surfaces. This file groups Firebase deploys, environment switching, data repair/backfill, App Check/security console work, CI gates, and App Store/TestFlight/Play release operations without changing the underlying tools.

operations.purpose: Select and verify the active Firebase/native app configuration before local builds, CI release builds, deploys, or console-driven checks, including consumer and host app roles.
requiredChecks: tool/validate_firebase_environment.sh <env> <consumer|host>
node tool/firebase/validate_google_maps_config.mjs --env <env> --platform ios
notes: Environment aliases are defined in .firebaserc.
Active checked-in config files are copied from firebase/<env> or firebase/<env>/host by tool/use_firebase_environment.sh.
The Android source-set google-services.json at android/app/src/<env>/ is also an active working copy and must match the selected app role.

operations.purpose: Deploy Firebase targets in a safe, explicit order with logical target expansion for Functions.
requiredChecks: node tool/contracts/check_firestore_contract.mjs
npm --prefix functions run lint
npm --prefix functions test
firebase emulators:exec --project demo-catch-rules --only firestore,storage "npm --prefix functions run test:rules"
notes: Known targets deploy in this order: functions, firestore:indexes, firestore:rules, storage, hosting, remoteconfig.
Extensions remain an explicit separate deploy path documented in docs/release_operations.md.

operations.purpose: Validate, repair, backfill, or intentionally delete Firestore data with dry-run defaults and apply guards.
requiredChecks: Prefer --json dry run before --apply.
Use --allow-prod only after reviewing prod dry-run output.
Validate a Supply Intake export without --apply, then repeat with explicit --environment, --project, and --confirm-run only to import the immutable internal shadow projection.
notes: Destructive tools require an explicit confirmation flag in addition to --apply.
Fraud/safety-related data changes should be tied back to backend_operation_catalog ownership before apply.
The Supply Intake projection importer writes only operationRuns and operationWorkItems; it cannot publish events or organizers, and a changed artifact requires a new run id.

operations.purpose: Export live admin organizer-intake curation and review decisions from Firestore into repo-backed projection batches before public website generation.
requiredChecks: Normalize newly discovered external surface URLs before adding intake batch or curation data.
Capture provider search results against a host-discovery run key before ingesting them as organizer evidence.
Run search-result ingestion after adding captured search batches and review matched/unknown surface candidates.
Capture external event payloads only from reviewed provider JSON; do not enable live crawling from this workflow.
Run external event-source ingestion after adding captured event batches and review every candidate before future import.
Publish generated Event Intake dashboard artifacts only to eventIntakeDashboards/current; do not use Marketing dashboards as the live Event Intake fallback.
Record reviewed event location resolutions through the admin bridge or repo-backed local drafts before event-source ingestion when exact coordinates exist.
Export reviewed event location resolutions before event-source ingestion when admin-resolved coordinates exist.
Review generated organizer_policy_gap_register.json before changing crawl, provider lookup, import write, defaults, or naming behavior.
Review generated organizer_policy_decision_packets.json and capture the required product inputs before encoding policy changes.
Record hold, accept, or reject policy-gap decisions before encoding crawl, provider lookup, import write, defaults, or naming policy changes.
Render generated organizer_pending_input_request.json before requesting admin or product decisions; it is the source of truth for pending human inputs.
Run pending-work coverage after operational-health changes; every unresolved workstream must be covered by a pending input or follow-up before promotion preview.
Create reviewed answer-packet copies with create_decision_answer_packet.mjs; never edit generated/organizer_pending_decision_answer_packet.json directly.
Run reviewed_decision_answer_packets.mjs --check before applying answer packets so absent, incomplete, stale, or invalid reviewed copies are visible.
Run pending_decision_answer_plan.mjs --require-complete against a reviewed copy before using --write-decision-answers.
Apply answered decision packets only through apply_pending_decision_answers.mjs or run_promotion_pipeline.mjs with explicit --write-decision-answers.
Run the event crawl run planner after organizer generation; treat it as a no-network run-intent artifact until crawl policy is approved.
Run the raw artifact storage planner after adding provider payloads; raw payloads must stay out of Firestore and remote upload remains disabled until bucket and retention policy are approved.
Run event location resolution planning after event-source ingestion; do not call Places or geocoding providers until provider policy and cost caps are approved.
Run the external event import planner after event-source ingestion; treat it as a no-write review artifact until import policy is approved.
Run the external event import execution preflight after import planning; every createEvent payload must validate before a future write-enabled importer exists.
Record event-candidate approve, hold, or reject decisions through the admin bridge or repo-backed event review batches before future import planning.
Record merge/suppress/surface curation before approving public projection when dedupe or false-positive evidence is present.
Run curation, organizer-review, event-review, and event-location exporters without --write first and review the dry-run output.
Run the policy-gap decision exporter without --write first and review the dry-run output before encoding policy changes.
Run node tool/organizer_intake/organizer_intake.mjs after writing an export.
Treat organizer_intake.mjs failures on exported approve_public decisions as publication blockers; the generator revalidates approvals against pre-approval packet readiness before projection.
Run node tool/organizer_intake/check_operational_health.mjs --check after generating organizer artifacts; p0_action_required is expected until admin and product inputs are recorded.
Review generated organizer_promotion_execution_packet.json before running remote exporters, website deploy, or claim-target sync.
Review generated canonical_host_entities.json before website projection, claim-target sync, crawl planning, or event import planning.
Review generated canonical_evidence_index.json before promoting pages, syncing claim targets, enabling crawl planning, or planning event imports.
Review generated publication_review_packets.json and use the packet-aware review_decision.mjs gate before recording approve, hold, or suppress publication decisions.
Review generated publication_decision_impact_preview.json before recording public approvals so index, claim-target, sitemap, and app-visibility consequences are explicit.
Review generated organizer_claim_target_sync_preview.json before running any live claim-target sync dry run or write.
Run npm --workspace catch-marketing run generate:organizer-listings after organizer projection changes.
Run node website/scripts/checkOrganizerBuildOutputs.mjs after marketing website builds to verify organizer route HTML, canonical tags, robots tags, and sitemap inclusion.
Run node tool/organizer_intake/check_admin_review_bridge.mjs before website deploy, claim-target sync, or adding a new review channel.
Run node tool/organizer_intake/check_promotion_bridge.mjs before website deploy or claim-target sync.

Use node tool/organizer_intake/run_promotion_pipeline.mjs for the local-only promotion preview, including search-result candidate regeneration and marketing website build-output validation, or with explicit env/write flags for reviewed remote operations.
Run claim-target sync without --write first and review creates/refreshes/skips before applying remote writes.
notes: The exporters read Firestore but never write remote data.
Platform adapters classify external URLs before manual review so dedupe keys and crawl defaults stay deterministic across search runs.
Search-result capture is local-only and requires a reviewed provider payload plus host-discovery run key; live provider selection and spend policy remain separate.
Search-result ingestion is local-only and produces review queues; it does not publish pages or mutate Firestore.
External event capture, ingestion, and review are scaffolding. Generated event candidates are blocked by policy and do not write events.
The policy gap register is declarative and does not enable crawls, provider lookups, or event writes.
The policy decision packet artifact is the operator-facing question list for unresolved product inputs; safe defaults keep behavior disabled.
Pending input and pending work coverage artifacts are read-only operator handoffs; they do not write Firestore or make policy choices.
Reviewed answer-packet registers are local-only readiness views over copied answer packets; stale copies must be recreated or explicitly overridden after manual comparison.
Applying an answer packet writes reviewed repo-backed decision JSON only after dry-running the exact local decision commands.
The canonical host registry is the private OrganizerEntity-to-public-Host boundary; legacy clubs are compatibility projections only.
The canonical evidence index ties host surfaces to source refs, local artifact hashes, raw payload storage status, and review state without writing remote data.
Publication review packets consolidate canonical host state, evidence, draft copy, gates, and admin decision commands; they do not publish or write remote data.
Publication decision impact previews simulate approval consequences without recording decisions, writing Firestore, publishing pages, or updating sitemaps.
The marketing postbuild step validates generated organizer route HTML and sitemap state before a website build succeeds.
Claim-target sync previews apply the same create/refresh/owner-bound skip rules as the sync tool against local fixture state and never read or write Firestore.
The event crawl run plan is local-only and never performs provider fetches, scheduled jobs, or Firestore writes.
The raw artifact storage manifest is local-only and never uploads files; it blocks raw provider payload storage until object-storage bucket, retention, deletion, and crawl-cost policy are approved.
Event location resolutions are manually reviewed coordinates from the admin bridge or repo-backed local drafts; provider lookup and recurring crawl remain disabled by policy.
External event import plans propose deterministic event write actions but do not mutate Firestore.
Publishing event supply readiness writes only eventSupplyReadiness/current for admin visibility; it does not import events or write externalEvents/{id}.
Policy gap decisions document product/ops review state only; they do not enable crawler, provider, or import behavior.
Event-candidate approval means approved for future import planning only; import writes remain disabled until product policy changes.
Live admin curation operations must be exported into reviewed repo-backed JSON before dedupe, public projection, and claim-target generation.
The admin review bridge checker proves every review channel has UI/API, callable, schema, Firestore export, tooling manifest, and promotion-pipeline wiring.
The promotion execution packet is the operator preflight for local generation, remote exports, website build validation, and claim-target sync; it does not execute those phases by itself.
The claim-target sync writes only approved public organizer projections into clubs/{entityId}.
Existing claimed or owner-bound organizer club documents are skipped by the sync tool.
Local JSON exports are intentionally reviewed in git before website deploy.
Exported approve_public decisions are rechecked against pre-approval publication packet readiness before any public projection or claim-target artifact is written.
Admin approval publishes and indexes website pages only after the static projection pipeline regenerates.
Approved public unclaimed pages can receive claim requests only after the claim-target sync creates or refreshes their Firestore club documents.

operations.purpose: Track App Check provider setup, callable enforcement, debug/release tokens, and abuse-control prerequisites that are partly managed in Firebase/GCP consoles.

entrypoints.ref: Firebase Console App Check provider registration and enforcement
entrypoints.guardrail: Record provider registration and enforcement evidence before release; no local script is allowed to claim console enforcement by itself.

entrypoints.ref: Firebase Console debug token registration for trusted devices
entrypoints.guardrail: Register debug tokens only for trusted test devices and remove stale tokens during release readiness review.

entrypoints.ref: Production admin Auth and App Check configuration
entrypoints.guardrail: After admin Auth, Hosting-domain, App Check-key, or provider changes, record console/API and live-browser evidence for authorized domains, Phone and Google providers, the App Check reCAPTCHA v3 server secret, and successful live App Check token exchange.

requiredChecks: functions/test/callable-app-check.test.cjs
tool/validate_firebase_environment.sh <env>
Console evidence for provider registration and enforcement before release or after Firebase app registration changes

For production admin changes, verify admin.catchdates.com and catchdates-admin.web.app as authorized Auth domains, Phone and Google providers enabled, the deployed reCAPTCHA v3 key paired with a Firebase App Check server secret, and successful live App Check token exchange before sign-in
notes: No local script should pretend to prove console enforcement by itself.
The debug-token helper only registers an explicitly configured local token for a selected Firebase app; it does not verify provider enforcement.
The manifest makes the manual console dependency explicit for future automation.
Host Android Firebase apps were registered on 2026-06-10 and mirror the matching consumer app SHA fingerprints for dev, staging, and prod.
Host App Check provider registrations were verified in Firebase Console on 2026-06-10 for dev, staging, and prod: Android uses Play Integrity, iOS uses App Attest, and web uses reCAPTCHA Enterprise.
Firebase CLI 15.20.0 and gcloud 566.0.0 do not expose App Check app-management commands in this local toolchain.

operations.purpose: Group the CI workflows that gate contracts, Functions, Firestore rules, app builds, tools, release readiness, and observability evidence.
requiredChecks: node tool/run.mjs check --manifest-only
node tool/check_remote_ops_manifest.mjs --check
notes: Workflow-specific proof still belongs in GitHub Actions run summaries.
This manifest is the local index for deciding which workflow owns which operational concern.
App Build Matrix includes host web, Android debug, and iOS simulator build coverage; Release Readiness can validate consumer, host, or both app roles.

operations.purpose: Track the manifest-owned Consumer/Host iOS TestFlight and Android Play-internal matrix, signed identity receipts, store authentication, legacy Xcode Cloud retirement, and guarded Play enablement.

entrypoints.ref: App Store Connect TestFlight group assignment and install/launch proof
entrypoints.guardrail: Both GitHub uploads processed and both legacy workflows are disabled; close APP-TARGET-IOS-GITHUB-CUTOVER-001 only after intended group assignment and install/launch proof exists.

entrypoints.ref: Google Play app records, app signing, publisher access, and internal testers
entrypoints.guardrail: Keep GOOGLE_PLAY_UPLOAD_ENABLED=false until both package records, qa tester lists, Play signing fingerprints, and least-privilege publisher access are verified.

requiredChecks: node tool/run.mjs check platform:app-targets platform:mobile-build-number platform:verify-ios-release-identity platform:verify-app-store-build platform:verify-ios-processing-receipts platform:verify-android-release-identity platform:probe-google-play-access

App-relevant main pushes fan out Consumer and Host on independent iOS and Android runners under approval-free, main-only prod-mobile credentials; reviewer-protected prod remains isolated for backend/data operations, and one global non-cancelling concurrency group prevents overlapping store edits.
Verify iOS archive/export identity plus App Store processing and Android AAB signature/certificate/compiled role/Firebase/Maps/version identity before claiming internal delivery.
Play publishing is limited to the qa track and forbidden for production.
Before enabling upload, a manual probe creates an edit, reads qa, and deletes the edit for both app records without uploading or committing.
Consumer requires HealthKit/Health Connect and public links; Host must not inherit them.
notes: GitHub Actions is canonical for both roles and both mobile platforms; public store promotion remains separate.
Mobile Internal Release preflights an 18-digit monotonic iOS candidate against App Store Connect and reserves two Android retry digits, then writes identity and processing receipts.

Mobile Internal Release run 29168074271 processed Consumer and Host build 202607110000000301 as VALID; retirement run 29168662623 verified those receipts and disabled both app-scoped Default workflows.
Apple Developer App ID Catch Host/com.catchdates.host and App Store Connect app id 6778927317 were created on 2026-06-10; SKU is catch-host-ios.
Legacy Catch | Default and Runner | Default Xcode Cloud workflows are disabled and retained only as rollback/audit surfaces.

Android Publisher API and the dedicated github-actions-play-publisher OIDC identity are ready with zero project roles; Play upload remains disabled until APP-TARGET-ANDROID-PLAY-001 closes app-record, app-scoped permission, App Signing, tester, and install proof.
App Store Connect displays an updated Apple Developer Program License Agreement notice that the Account Holder should accept before treating host uploads/submissions as release-ready.
App Store review metadata and TestFlight group membership remain console-owned.
